column1: What is the 8- to 16-cell stage of the cleaving embryo called?

column2: The thin portion of endometrium between the implantation site and the uterine lumen.

column1: What is the name of the remaining endometrium of the uterus that is not at the implantation site?
Which region of the chorion contains the highly branched villi adjacent to the decidua basalis?

column1: What is the term for the 15 to 25 partially separated compartments of the mature placenta?

column2: The invasion of extraembryonic mesenchyme into the core.

column1: What structural element characterizes a tertiary chorionic villus?
column2: The presence of fetal blood vessels in the connective tissue core.

column1: Fetal blood and maternal blood are separated by the _____.
Which multinucleated layer lines the intervillous space and rests on the trophoblast basal lamina?
What are Hofbauer cells?
What transition occurs in the placental barrier starting at the fourth month to facilitate exchange?
column2: The cytotrophoblast layer becomes thin and discontinuous.

column1: How many umbilical arteries are typically present in the umbilical cord?
Fetal blood returns from the placenta to the fetus through a single _____.

What is the approximate total surface area of the villi in a mature human placenta?

Which hormone, synthesized by the syncytiotrophoblast, is the basis for most pregnancy tests?

column2: It regulates glucose metabolism and stimulates mammary duct proliferation.

column1: By the end of which week does the placenta produce enough progesterone to maintain pregnancy without the corpus luteum?

Which condition is characterized by clear, swollen villi often resulting from trophoblastic disease?

Dizygotic twins always develop with separate _____.
What is the clue for distinguishing an amniotic septum from a chorionic septum in twins?
column2: Amniotic membranes are transparent; chorionic membranes are thicker and opaque.

column1: What happens to the chorionic cavity by the eighth week of development?
column2: It is obliterated as the amniotic cavity fills the chorionic space.

column1: Which fetal cells synthesize $IGF-I$ and $IGF-II$ to stimulate their own proliferation?

column2: Villi that extend from the chorionic plate to the maternal side to make contact with tissue.

column1: What are 'free villi'?
column2: Villi that arborize within the placental substance without anchoring to the maternal side.

column1: What structure connects the midgut of the embryo to the yolk sac?

column2: A thin layer of cytotrophoblastic cells formed around the syncytiotrophoblast.

column1: Which growth factor stimulates the function of the differentiated trophoblast in the 6- to 12-week placenta?
Where are primordial germ cells first identifiable in humans?
column2: The wall of the yolk sac

column1: How does $hPL$ promote growth of the fetus metabolically?
column2: It converts the mother to a fatty acid user, sparing carbohydrates for the fetus.

column1: The umbilical ring surrounds the roots of which two structures?

column2: Late splitting of the bilaminar embryonic disc

column1: What are 'placental septae' made of?

What is the thickness of a mature placenta at term?

column1: What happens to the vitelline duct normally by birth?
